evidence_quotes.quote: Google is planning to only allowed installation of apps that are signed by them even if they are published outside the Play Store
Apple not only requires apps to be signed by them but also restricts the number of apps from outside the App Store that can be installed at the same time.
Google's new requirement that apps be signed by them can kill apps like F-Droid which distribute open-source software.
most smartphone manufacturers lock the device's bootloader so that installing alternative operating systems is impossible

and even if the bootloader is unlocked, some apps use hardware attestation using APIs such as Google Play Integrity so that it's impossible to run them in an alternative operating system unless the app developer officially supports them

Google Play Integrity also blocks running some apps while the owner of the device has the ability to run apps using the root account, which gives the owner total control over his own device to do things that Google does not normally allow users to do on their own device.

I hope governments around the world at the very least regulate the use of hardware attestation such that people are not forced to use a smartphone running Google's Android or Apple's iOS that restricts what they can do with their own phone.

paragraphs.text: Smartphones are computers, yet corporations are controlling what software can be installed to smartphones by their owners. Google is planning to only allowed installation of apps that are signed by them even if they are published outside the Play Store, and Apple not only requires apps to be signed by them but also restricts the number of apps from outside the App Store that can be installed at the same time. Google's new requirement that apps be signed by them can kill apps like F-Droid which distribute open-source software. It is currently impossible for any competitor to Android and iOS to compete, because most smartphone manufacturers lock the device's bootloader so that installing alternative operating systems is impossible, and even if the bootloader is unlocked, some apps use hardware attestation using APIs such as Google Play Integrity so that it's impossible to run them in an alternative operating system unless the app developer officially supports them, and most developers will not support an alternative operating system with a low market share. Google Play Integrity also blocks running some apps while the owner of the device has the ability to run apps using the root account, which gives the owner total control over his own device to do things that Google does not normally allow users to do on their own device. Google and app developers want corporations to control what people can do with their own smartphones. Currently many essential apps such as banking apps, payment apps and even apps provided by governments use Google Play Integrity so that running them in alternative operating systems based on Android or Linux is impossible. Some banking and government apps even require people to use these apps and do not have a web portal that can be used in any device, or the web portal requires use of the smartphone app (and not a third-party TOTP app like Google Authenticator) for two factor authentication. This gives Google and Apple an eternal duopoly over the smartphone market. I hope governments around the world at the very least regulate the use of hardware attestation such that people are not forced to use a smartphone running Google's Android or Apple's iOS that restricts what they can do with their own phone.
